page.title: Princess V58 vs Fairline Targa 12m: Marbella Compared

data.meta_description: Compare Princess V58 vs Fairline Targa 12m for Marbella charters. See specs, prices, and which suits your group. Book your boat today.
data.summary: A head-to-head comparison of the Princess V58 and Fairline Targa 12m for boat charters in Marbella. Discover key specs, pricing, and which vessel is best for your group size and itinerary.
key_facts: Princess V58: 18.3m, up to 12 pax, departs Puerto Banús
Fairline Targa 12m: 12m, up to 10 pax, departs Puerto Banús
Fairline Targa 12m is more agile and fuel-efficient for short trips
Princess V58 offers more deck space and luxury for larger groups
Both include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and IVA

faq.q: Which boat is faster, Princess V58 or Fairline Targa 12m?
faq.a: The Princess V58 reaches a top speed of 35 knots, while the Fairline Targa 12m tops out at 38 knots. For shorter trips, the Targa is quicker. For longer cruises, the V58 offers a smoother ride at cruising speeds around 28 knots.

faq.q: Can I charter both boats for a full day in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes, both are available for 8-hour charters. The Princess V58 costs €4,799/8h, and the Fairline Targa 12m costs €2,999/8h. Both include a skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT.

faq.q: Which boat is better for a party?
faq.a: The Princess V58 has a larger flybridge and aft deck, ideal for up to 12 guests. The Fairline Targa 12m has a sportier layout with a sun pad and cockpit, better for 8–10 people wanting a lively but compact space.

faq.q: Do both boats leave from Puerto Banús?
faq.a: Yes, both the Princess V58 and Fairline Targa 12m depart from Puerto Banús. This gives you quick access to Marbella's coastline, Cabopino, and Estepona.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price for each boat?
faq.a: Both charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and 21% Spanish IVA. No hidden extras.

The Fairline Targa 12m is more fuel-efficient due to its smaller size and lighter displacement. For a 4-hour trip, expect lower fuel costs compared to the Princess V58, though fuel is included in the charter price.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.a: Yes, you can bring your own food and drinks on both boats. The standard package already includes drinks and snacks, but you're welcome to supplement. The crew will store and serve your items.

faq.q: Which boat is better for a family with children?
faq.a: Both are safe for families, but the Princess V58 offers more shaded areas and a larger cabin, making it more comfortable for children. The Fairline Targa 12m has open decks that are better for older kids who want to swim.

data.body_html:
Choosing between the Princess V58 and the Fairline Targa 12m for your Marbella boat charter? Both are premium vessels that depart from Puerto Banús, but they suit different needs. This head-to-head comparison covers specs, pricing, and real-world use so you can pick the right boat for your group. For a full overview of our fleet, visit the Boat Rental Marbella hub.

Princess V58 vs Fairline Targa 12m: Key Specs

SpecPrincess V58Fairline Targa 12m
Length18.3m12m
Max passengers1210
Top speed35 knots38 knots
Cruising speed28 knots30 knots
Departure pointPuerto BanúsPuerto Banús

The Princess V58 is larger and more spacious, while the Fairline Targa 12m is nimbler and faster. Both are fully equipped with licensed skippers, fuel, drinks, and snacks included.

Pricing: What You Pay

Pricing differs significantly. The Princess V58 starts at €1,499 for 2 hours and goes up to €4,799 for 8 hours. The Fairline Targa 12m is more affordable: €999 for 2 hours and €2,999 for 8 hours. Both include VAT, skipper, fuel, and refreshments. For budget-conscious groups, the Targa offers great value. For a splurge, the V58 delivers extra luxury.

Space and Comfort

The Princess V58 has a large flybridge with seating for 8, a spacious aft deck, and a sun pad forward. The interior cabin is air-conditioned with a galley and seating. The Fairline Targa 12m has a sportier layout: a cockpit with L-shaped seating, a sun pad on the foredeck, and a compact cabin below. For 10 guests, the Targa feels cosy; for 12, the V58 is better.

If you're planning a day trip with a larger group, the V58's extra space makes a difference. For a couple or small family, the Targa is more intimate.

Performance and Handling

The Fairline Targa 12m is lighter and faster, reaching 38 knots. It handles well in light seas and is fun for water sports. The Princess V58 is heavier and more stable at cruising speed, ideal for longer passages to Estepona or Sotogrande. Both boats are easy to drive, but the Targa's smaller size makes it more manoeuvrable in Marbella Marina.

Best Uses: Which Boat for Which Trip?

  • Princess V58: Best for full-day charters, corporate events, or luxury sunset cruises. Ideal for groups of 10–12 wanting space and comfort.
  • Fairline Targa 12m: Great for half-day trips, water sports, or a quick escape to Cabopino. Suits groups of 6–10 who value speed and affordability.

Both boats can anchor at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés. The V58 has a larger swim platform for easy access to the water.

What's Included in Every Charter

All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and 21% Spanish IVA. No hidden costs. You can bring your own food and drinks—the crew will store them.

Which One Should You Book?

If you want maximum space and luxury for a full day, choose the Princess V58. If you prefer a sporty, fast boat for a shorter trip at a lower price, the Fairline Targa 12m is the better pick. Both are excellent vessels and will give you a memorable day on the Costa del Sol.

Browse our full fleet at the Boat Rental Marbella homepage or check out our blog for more comparisons.

Frequently asked questions

Which boat is faster, Princess V58 or Fairline Targa 12m?

The Princess V58 reaches a top speed of 35 knots, while the Fairline Targa 12m tops out at 38 knots. For shorter trips, the Targa is quicker. For longer cruises, the V58 offers a smoother ride at cruising speeds around 28 knots.

Can I charter both boats for a full day in Marbella?

Yes, both are available for 8-hour charters. The Princess V58 costs €4,799/8h, and the Fairline Targa 12m costs €2,999/8h. Both include a skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT.

Which boat is better for a party?

The Princess V58 has a larger flybridge and aft deck, ideal for up to 12 guests. The Fairline Targa 12m has a sportier layout with a sun pad and cockpit, better for 8–10 people wanting a lively but compact space.

Do both boats leave from Puerto Banús?

Yes, both the Princess V58 and Fairline Targa 12m depart from Puerto Banús. This gives you quick access to Marbella's coastline, Cabopino, and Estepona.

What is included in the charter price for each boat?

Both charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and 21% Spanish IVA. No hidden extras.

Which boat is more fuel-efficient?

The Fairline Targa 12m is more fuel-efficient due to its smaller size and lighter displacement. For a 4-hour trip, expect lower fuel costs compared to the Princess V58, though fuel is included in the charter price.

Can I bring my own food and drinks?

Yes, you can bring your own food and drinks on both boats. The standard package already includes drinks and snacks, but you're welcome to supplement. The crew will store and serve your items.

Which boat is better for a family with children?

Both are safe for families, but the Princess V58 offers more shaded areas and a larger cabin, making it more comfortable for children. The Fairline Targa 12m has open decks that are better for older kids who want to swim.
